name: Matzoh Ball Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 small stalks celery, cut into 3-inch pieces
1 sprig fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken in a large soup pot. Cover with water and bring to a boil.
2. Lower the heat to a simmer and skim as needed. Simmer for 10 minutes.
3. Add vegetables, herbs, salt, and pepper. Bring back to a boil and simmer partially covered for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Strain the broth through a fine mesh sieve, removing any fat from the surface, and reduce to about 6 cups to concentrate flavor.
5. In a bowl, mix beaten eggs, seltzer water, melted chicken fat, salt, pepper, and matzah meal until well combined. Chill, covered, in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours and up to 8 hours.
6. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Using cool, wet hands, shape the matzoh mixture into eight balls about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
7. Reduce the heat to simmering and gently add the matzoh balls. Cover the pot and poach the matzoh balls in the barely simmering water for 25 minutes.
8. Add matzoh balls to the soup and serve.
